data.narrative:
Rigetti Computing is a public-stage quantum computing company currently valued at $5.5B. The valuation landscape for quantum computing includes 5 same-sector, same-stage peers: IonQ ($11.0B), Quantinuum ($10.0B), D-Wave Quantum ($2.0B), Infleqtion ($1.8B), and Archer Materials ($60M), creating a wide dispersion across the sector.

The peer-median method, applied across these 5 public quantum computing companies, yields an implied valuation of $2.0B. The median anchors the center of the peer distribution, which ranges from $60M at the low end (Archer Materials) to $11.0B at the high end (IonQ). This method is the primary triangulation lens available given the data constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B to $2.3B, centered at $2.0B—approximately 64% below Rigetti's current $5.5B valuation. The wide peer range ($60M to $11.0B) reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity, commercialization stage, and market positioning within quantum computing. Key uncertainties include Rigetti's specific technical differentiation relative to the peer set, revenue traction versus pre-revenue peers, and sector-specific valuation multiples not captured in peer medians. A single methodology limits confidence; additional revenue or EBITDA multiple approaches would strengthen conviction in any conclusion.
